arts.sum: Empoli have won eight of their 22 Serie A matches (D5 L9) against Napoli: against no other team have the Toscani picked up more wins in the competition (also eight vs Torino and Cagliari). Empoli have won five of their last seven Serie A games (L2) against Napoli, included both last season (both 1-0), after suffering four defeats in a row against the Campani.
Napoli will aim to strengthen their position as Serie A league leaders when they face Empoli at the Stadio Carlo Castellani this noon. Napoli head into this match on the…

arts.ttl: Devis Vasquez: “In England they are disorganised. I am ready for Milan.”
arts.sum: Devis Vasquez, the Colombian goalkeeper currently making waves at Empoli, has quickly transformed from an unknown entity to a potential phenomenon in Italian football. In an exclusive interview with La Gazzetta dello Sport, the 26-year-old shot-stopper discusses his journey, experiences, and aspirations in the world of football. When asked about his sudden rise to prominence at Empoli, Vasquez reflects, “I’ve been in Europe for a year and a half. I’ve been through Milan, Sheffield, and Ascoli. I’ve learned a lot. I’ve gained experience, which has served me well here at Empoli, where I’ve finally been given the opportunity to start. The post Devis Vasquez: “In England they are disorganised. I am ready for Milan.” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Empoli v Napoli: Match Preview
arts.sum: Serie A leaders Napoli face a tricky test on Sunday as they travel to Stadio Carlo Castellani to take on a stubborn Empoli side. The lunchtime clash pits Antonio Conte’s high-flying Partenopei against Roberto D’Aversa’s well-organized Azzurri in what promises to be an intriguing tactical battle. Napoli enter the match in imperious form, having won six of their last seven matches across all competitions. Their 3-1 victory over Como before the international break extended their unbeaten run to six games and kept them two points clear of reigning champions Inter Milan at the Serie A summit. arts.ttl: Napoli brace for Kvaratskhelia’s potential summer departure as contract talks stall
arts.sum: Napoli are coming to terms with the possibility of losing their star winger Khvicha Kvaratskhelia in the upcoming summer transfer window. TMW report how contract renewal negotiations with the Georgian international have been ongoing for over a year, but an agreement remains elusive. Initially, there seemed to be progress when Napoli president Aurelio De Laurentiis traveled to Germany during Georgia’s national team camp. A proposal of around 5 million euros per year for a five-year contract was reportedly discussed. However, the latest meeting between the parties ended in a stalemate.
